text: Hi, this is Bridget Singleton. Do you ship the halibut order overnight or is it standard 2-day?
Hi Bridget, it goes out overnight on ice packs so it lands fresh. Ships tomorrow AM once we're back at dock.
Perfect, that works. Please send tracking once it's out. Thanks!
Hi, this is Patrick Singleton. The bait cooler I bought off you last week has a cracked latch, I'd like to return it.
Hey Patrick, sorry to hear that. Bring it by the dock whenever, I'll refund or swap it for you no problem.
Appreciate it, I'll drop it off tomorrow morning around 8. Thanks for making it easy.
Hi, just confirming we're still on for the striper trip Saturday at 6am out of the Bay Runner?
Yes Siobhan, you're all set for Sat 6/6 at 6am, dock B. Bring sunscreen, I've got rods/bait covered.
Perfect, thank you! See you then.
Hi, what are F/V Bay Runner's store hours this week? Need to grab bait before we head out.
Hi Kathleen! We're open 6am-5pm today, dock's open a bit later if you need fuel too.
Perfect, I'll swing by around 4. Thanks!
Hey, this is Brian Singleton. Do you have any fresh halibut left from today's catch?
Hey Brian, F/V Bay Runner here. Yep, got about 40 lbs of halibut still iced down at the dock.
Perfect, save me 10 lbs. I'll swing by the dock around 5pm to grab it.
Hey it's Frank Singleton, got the Zelle receipt but it doesn't show what it was for. Was that for the halibut order or the deposit on next trip?
Hey Frank, that was the $180 deposit for the Aug 14 trip. Halibut from last week was cash, already settled.
Got it, thanks for clearing that up. Can you shoot me a text receipt for the deposit for my records?
Frank, layout boat looks good for Saturday. Tide's right, wind out of the NW.
Decoys are loaded in the truck. Want me to grab coffee on the way?
Kathleen's fridge is stocked, checked it Sunday. She's doing fine.
Goose numbers were thin last week but the weather's turning our way.
Bring the thermos, gonna be a cold one out on the Shore.
You still good to run the Eastern Shore blind Nov 14?
Kathleen asked about you at dinner. Told her you'd swing by.
Tommy, need 50 more pots before Friday. Razor clams running low too.
You good to run the Saturday haul while I'm at Connor's game?
Cull board caught a bunch of sooks today, tossed em back.
Fuel dock opens at 5, meet you at Slip 47.
Charter party of 6 tomorrow, rig for rockfish.
Weather's laying down after noon, we push the second string then.
Good haul today, close to four bushels a pot line.
Bait order's in, arrives Thursday. Bull lip and menhaden.
Kevin, we on for open mic Tuesday? Bringing the Martin.
Thinking we open with that Jamey Johnson tune, then some Petty.
Bay Red Ale's carbonating nice, bringing a growler.
You want to run the Waylon number in G or drop it to E?
Dockside's got the back room for us again, 7:30.
Amp's buzzing, might swap the tube before Tuesday.
D.J., 4:30 at the dock, we're pulling the north string first.
Nice work on the cull today, you're getting quick.
Bring gloves, supposed to be raw out there tomorrow.
Payroll hits Friday, same as always through Annapolis Payroll.
Mike, charter's a family of 5, be ready to help with the kids' lines.
You did good with the customers Saturday, they tipped well.
Meet at the dock 5 sharp, half-day trip.
Pete, open Saturday June 27 for a full day if the party's still on.
Rockfish been biting off Thomas Point, tell the clients.
Got two half-days free the week of the 4th if you need slots.
Rate sheet looks good, send it over and I'll confirm.
Patrick, you're on the boat Saturday, summer deckhand rate.
Nice game last night, that clear in the fourth was clutch.
We'll do the solo sign-off drive once you're 17, promise.
Grab your waders from the garage, they're by the freezer.
Connor's game moved to 9 sharp Saturday, Annapolis Rec fields.
Picking up Kathleen Sunday morning, back by 1 for dinner.
Siobhan's regatta is at Severn Sailing this weekend.
Grabbing crabs from the boat for Sunday dinner, no screens at the table.
Homework's done, Connor's in bed. Long day.
Cantler's for your birthday Nov 18? Booked if you want crabs.
Duke got into the trash again, cleaned it up.
Patrick aced his driving test, kid's grinning ear to ear.
